note: Read-only consistency LINT of the whole ADR corpus (dangling refs, numbering holes, duplicate numbers, broken supersede pairs, placeholder residue, invalid statuses, stale index). Distinct from adr-coverage (what's UNDOCUMENTED vs the stack), adr-review (one proposed ADR), and adr (drafting). mock_rate encodes the ideal outcome for --decider mock; real deciders ignore it.

queries.text: audit the ADRs
run an ADR health check before we cut the release
are our decision records consistent? feels like some references are dangling
lint the whole ADR corpus under docs/adr and tell me what's broken
check the ADRs for numbering holes and duplicate numbers
something's off with our supersede pairs — a couple ADRs say superseded but I can't find the successor. can you sweep the corpus?
some of the ADRs still have those {placeholder} slots and weird statuses like 'draft' — find all of them
my manager wants a decision-records consistency report, is the ADR index stale?
sanity-check every file in docs/adr — invalid statuses, unparseable records, broken cross-refs, the lot
do an ADR lint pass, we haven't checked them in months and I bet the numbering has gaps
what architecture decisions are we missing? compare the ADRs against our actual stack
queries.note: gap analysis of undocumented decisions → adr-coverage

queries.text: review ADR 31 before we ratify it — is it ready?
queries.note: deep review of ONE proposed record → adr-review

queries.text: write an ADR for the switch to Postgres
mark ADR 0018 as accepted, it's approved now
supersede ADR 0007 with a new record and link them both ways
regenerate the Architecture decisions digest in CLAUDE.md from the accepted ADRs
where's the ADR that explains why we went single-server? point me to it
explain the reasoning in ADR-0029, walk me through the refactor family decision
queries.note: explain rationale of one record → explain

queries.text: run a full security audit of the codebase before prod
queries.note: shares 'audit' but it's a security scan → sec-scan

queries.text: the ADR numbering is a mess — renumber 0012 and 0013 so there are no gaps
queries.note: asks to PERFORM renumbering edits; audit is read-only and reports, doesn't renumber → manual edit / adr tool

queries.text: are the ADRs internally consistent — dangling references, orphaned supersede links, that kind of thing?
queries.note: internal-consistency lint, not stack-vs-docs gaps — must beat adr-coverage

queries.text: before I ratify anything, sweep the whole corpus for broken records and placeholder residue
queries.note: corpus-wide lint despite 'ratify' vocab — must beat adr-review (which is one record)

queries.text: which architecture decisions in our infra and CI are undocumented? rank them by blast radius
queries.note: gap analysis of missing decisions vs the stack — must lose to adr-coverage

queries.text: check whether ADR 0031 is ready to accept — sections filled, claims grounded, links resolve
queries.note: single-record readiness review — must lose to adr-review
